3. Try MOUSE to click somewhere
Let me also look at the sky blue path more carefully to understand where the agent can go. The agent is at rows 37-40, cols 32-34. Looking at the ascii, row 41 has "GNNNGBSBBBS" - there's an S at col 31. But the agent is above that.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check sky blue path nodes
seg = current_frame.segmentation
s_nodes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'S']
print(f"Sky blue nodes: {len(s_nodes)}")
for n in s_nodes:
    print(f"  S node: boundary={n['boundary']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, children={n['children']}")

# Check agent adjacency
agent_b = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'b'][0]
agent_Y = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'Y'][0]
adj = seg['adjacency_list']
print(f"\nAgent b (id={agent_b['id']}) adjacencies:")
for a in adj:
    if a[0] == agent_b['id'] or a[1] == agent_b['id']:
        other_id = a[1] if a[0] == agent_b['id'] else a[0]
        other = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['id'] == other_id][0]
        print(f"  -> {other['color']} (id={other_id})")
print(f"Agent Y (id={agent_Y['id']}) adjacencies:")
for a in adj:
    if a[0] == agent_Y['id'] or a[1] == agent_Y['id']:
        other_id = a[1] if a[0] == agent_Y['id'] else a[0]
        other = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['id'] == other_id][0]
        print(f"  -> {other['color']} (id={other_id})")

# Look at ascii around agent
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nAscii around agent (rows 35-45, cols 25-45):")
for r in range(35, min(46, len(ascii_lines))):
    print(f"  {r}: {ascii_lines[r][25:45]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
